journal_entry.description: Sale of 300 cases of snack products, partial cash payment
journal_entry.rationale: Revenue recognized at $69,000 for 300 cases sold. Cash received $45,900, remainder $30,000 on credit (accounts receivable). Sales tax of $6,900 (10% per transaction) collected and recorded as HST Payable. Cost of goods sold is $55,500 (300 cases × $185), reducing inventory.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Sale of inventory: two asset increases (cash $45,900 + receivable $30,000), one expense increase (COGS $55,500), one liability increase (HST payable $6,900), one revenue increase (sales $69,000), one asset decrease (inventory $55,500). Transaction states 10% tax rate explicitly, so used as stated. Debits = $45,900 + $30,000 + $55,500 = $131,400; Credits = $6,900 + $69,000 + $55,500 = $131,400. Balanced.
